what_it_does: This file is an index and durable record of the Company's autonomous build-loop recipes, explaining that copies of the live skills from ~/.claude/skills/ are stored in the repo so the methodology travels with the project, and it lists each recipe with a short description of what it builds.
observations: The recipes are described as cron-driven, parallel, crash-safe loops that read a plan, build disjoint lanes in parallel, verify by use plus adversarial re-check, commit per criterion on a branch, and flag items for the operator.
The file explicitly frames its purpose as closing a durability gap: saved methodology files in ~/.claude/skills/ are not version-controlled, so copies are placed in the repo.
It lists five recipes in a table: company-build, rhm-build, wire-build, remediation-build, and loop-prep, plus plan-review.
company-build is described as the recipe from which the others descend.
remediation-build is singled out as verifying the seam, not the cell.
The live runnable versions remain in ~/.claude/skills/; these repo copies are the durable record and reference.
When a recipe changes, the file advises updating both copies or re-copying the live skill into the repo.
standouts: It treats local Claude skills as source material and copies them into the repo to make the methodology durable and version-controlled.
It describes the build loops as autonomous, cron-driven, parallel, crash-safe, and verified by actual use.
It distinguishes remediation verification as seam-level rather than cell-level.
conventions: Uses a README as an index/table-of-contents for a set of related documents.
Uses backticks for recipe filenames and code-like terms.
Includes a parenthetical note about source-of-truth and synchronization between live and durable copies.
Uses a two-column table to map recipe names to their responsibilities.
concerns: It assumes the reader already knows what ~/.claude/skills/ is and why it matters.
Several terms, such as 'operable composition surface' and 'RHM walkthrough & review organ', are not defined here.
It does not provide the actual repo paths to the listed recipe files.
The dual-source live/durable copy arrangement creates a synchronization risk that the file only mentions in passing.
notes: This README is a map and durability note for the Company's build-loop recipe collection, not a runnable recipe itself.
questions: Where in the repo are the actual recipe files (company-build.md, rhm-build.md, etc.) located?
What is 'RHM' and what does the RHM walkthrough & review organ do?
What does 'operable composition surface' mean in this context?
How is the cron-driven loop actually scheduled and executed?
What does 'verify by use' and 'adversarial re-check' look like in practice?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: it states it is a durable copy and index of build-loop recipes, and that is exactly what it does, listing each recipe and noting the live/durable copy relationship.
apparent_themes: Verification by use rather than inspection alone
Operator escalation for what the loop cannot resolve
intention_signals: It is trying to make the project's methodology version-controlled and portable by mirroring the operator's local skill files into the repository and giving each recipe a discoverable landing page.
novelty: The unusual part is framing build methodology as copied 'skills' from a local Claude skills directory stored in the repo to close a 'saved ≠ in the system' durability gap, and describing recipes as cron-driven, parallel, crash-safe loops that verify by use.
contribution: It contributes a durable, version-controlled index of the build-loop recipes and their intended responsibilities, preserving the methodology alongside the codebase and documenting the relationship between live skill copies and repo copies.
summary_for_embedding: The methodology README is a durable, version-controlled index of the Company's autonomous build-loop recipe files, explaining their origin in ~/.claude/skills/, their cron-driven parallel crash-safe structure, and what each recipe builds.
intention_for_embedding: This file exists to close the durability gap between live local skills and the project repository by providing a discoverable, in-repo record of the build-loop recipes and their roles.
